nodes.name: Black-fronted white-eye
nodes.summary: The black-fronted white-eye (Zosterops chrysolaemus) is a songbird species. It is closely related to the Old World babblers, and its family Zosteropidae might better be included in the Tiimalidae. The black-fronted white-eye was formerly considered a subspecies of the green-fronted white-eye (Zosterops minor), and until it was split as a distinct species, the name "black-fronted white-eye" was also used for Z. minor. It is found in New Guinea. Quite common, it is considered of least concern by the IUCN. References External links Species fact sheet by BirdLife
